description_long: This object is a pair of metal pliers with a classic, utilitarian design, commonly found in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or toolboxes. The pliers feature two curved handles that converge at a central pivot, allowing the jaws to open and close with a scissor-like motion. The jaws are robust and slightly serrated, designed for gripping, twisting, or cutting wires and other materials. The surface of the pliers appears to be made of steel, exhibiting a matte, slightly rough finish with visible wear and texture. The handles are not insulated and have a simple, ribbed pattern for improved grip. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool, emphasizing its generic, functional nature.
description: A sturdy, metal pair of pliers with curved handles and serrated jaws, featuring a matte, slightly rough finish and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: A narrow side profile of the pliers, showing the thinness of the handles and jaws.
description_view_1: A full frontal view displaying the curved handles, central pivot, and serrated jaws in detail.
description_view_2: A narrow side profile similar to view 0, emphasizing the slim construction of the tool.
description_view_3: A full rear view, mirroring the frontal view, with clear visibility of the handles and jaw alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, non-insulated metal handles with a basic ergonomic arc.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, robust jaws with a flat, serrated gripping surface and a central notch for wire cutting.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot, allowing standard scissor-like motion without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Ribbed metal texture along the handles for improved grip.
